methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Cortical Labs is a Series A-stage AI & Software company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage companies: Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M), supplemented by reference to a broader private and public comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech firms and one public peer (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to the $11M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $22M. The peer-median method uses the median valuation of the three identified comps, which stands at $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M (floor) to $2.4B (ceiling). These methods produce divergent signals reflecting the heterogeneity within early-stage AI funding: the stage-progression method suggests a lower anchor tied to capital deployed, while peer comparables reflect the broader market's willingness to value frontier-AI teams at substantial premiums early in their lifecycle.

The triangulated analysis supports a range from $19M (conservative) to $2.3B (peer-driven ceiling), with a median point of $1.0B. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of stage-progression versus peer valuation given wide dispersion in the comparable set, and whether Cortical Labs' specific technical positioning and founding team warrant positioning toward the peer median or the lower end of the range.
